prerequisites: A running PostgreSQL instance (Postgres 13+) reachable from the connector, with connection details: host, port (default 5432), database name, username, and password.
The pgvector extension installed at the server level and enabled in the target database with: CREATE EXTENSION vector;
A database user with permission to create objects in the target schema (the public schema is used by default; schema names are case sensitive).
An account and API key for the chosen embedding provider (OpenAI or Cohere), unless using Fake embeddings for testing.

authMethods.name: PostgreSQL database password (indexing.credentials.password)
authMethods.summary: The destination authenticates to the target PostgreSQL database using the configured username and password supplied under the indexing connection settings. The password is the secret field indexing.credentials.password.

authMethods.name: OpenAI API key (embedding.openai_key)
authMethods.summary: When the OpenAI embedding method is selected, provide an OpenAI API key in embedding.openai_key. OpenAI uses the text-embedding-ada-002 model producing 1536-dimension vectors.

authMethods.name: Cohere API key (embedding.cohere_key)
authMethods.summary: When the Cohere embedding method is selected, provide a Cohere API key in embedding.cohere_key. Cohere uses the embed-english-light-v2.0 model producing 1024-dimension vectors.

authMethods.name: Generic embedding API key (embedding.api_key)
authMethods.summary: For embedding methods that take a generic key (such as Azure OpenAI or an OpenAI-compatible endpoint), provide the key in embedding.api_key. Not required when using Fake embeddings.

setupSteps.title: Provision PostgreSQL and enable pgvector
setupSteps.body: Use a PostgreSQL 13+ instance and enable the vector extension in the target database by running CREATE EXTENSION vector; The extension must already be installed at the server level (available via Docker, package managers, or a hosted Postgres provider) before it can be enabled.

setupSteps.title: Create a database user and grant access
setupSteps.body: Create or choose a database user the connector will use, and grant it the privileges needed to create and write to objects in the target schema, for example: CREATE USER airbyte_user WITH PASSWORD '<password>'; then GRANT CREATE, TEMPORARY ON DATABASE <database> TO airbyte_user; You will supply this username and password in the indexing configuration.

setupSteps.title: Configure the embedding method
setupSteps.body: Set the embedding config field to your chosen method. For OpenAI, supply embedding.openai_key. For Cohere, supply embedding.cohere_key. For Azure OpenAI or OpenAI-compatible endpoints, supply embedding.api_key. Fake embeddings require no key and are intended only for testing. The embedding dimensions must match the vector column the connector creates.

setupSteps.title: Configure indexing (Postgres connection)
setupSteps.body: Set the indexing config field with the database connection: host, port (default 5432), database name (DB Name), username, password (indexing.credentials.password), and default schema (the public schema is used by default; schema names are case sensitive).

setupSteps.title: Configure processing and raw text handling
setupSteps.body: Set the processing config field to control how records are split and embedded: specify the text fields to embed (dot notation such as user.name and wildcards such as users.*.name are supported), optional metadata fields used only for filtering (string, number, boolean, or list-of-string types), and the chunk size in tokens (maximum 8191). Optionally enable omit_raw_text to avoid storing the original text alongside the vectors.

sources.title: pgvector/pgvector - Open-source vector similarity search for Postgres
